methods.description: Characterization of photosynthetic parameters based on chlorophyll a variable fluorescence using a Kolber bench-top fast repetition rate fluorometer (FRRF). This method involves specific excitation protocols, blank measurements, sample handling, environmental control, and subsequent data analysis to calculate parameters like maximum quantum yield efficiency (Fv/Fm) and functional absorption cross sections (σPSII).

steps.description: Photosynthetic parameters based on chlorophyll a variable fluorescence were characterized using a Kolber bench-top fast repetition rate fluorometer (FRRF; [20]).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up and operate Kolber bench-top fast repetition rate fluorometer (FRRF)

steps.description: The measurement protocol was optimized to obtain fluorescence saturation (Fm) by a rapid sequence of 80 flashlets, followed by 30 flashlets for the relaxation portion.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply FRR excitation protocol with specific flashlet sequences
inputs: Measurement protocol parameters (80 flashlets for saturation, 30 for relaxation)

steps.description: Instrument blanks were determined with distilled water to assess light scattering within the cuvette, as well as seawater that had been filtered through a Millex AA 0.8 µm Millipore membrane to account for fluorescence of dissolved organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure instrument blanks using distilled water and filtered seawater

steps.description: Samples were either placed into the cuvette via pipette or dispensed with a peristaltic pump (maximum flow rate was 5 mL min -1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Introduce sample into the FRRF cuvette

steps.description: To minimize condensation on the exterior of the cuvette, the light and cuvette chamber were constantly flushed with dry nitrogen gas.
steps.searchable_summary: Flush FRRF chamber with dry nitrogen gas

steps.description: The maximum quantum yield efficiency for PSII (Fv/Fm) was calculated [21] by normalizing Fm by the difference between the fluorescence at saturation (F m ) and the minimum fluorescence (F o ): Fv/Fm = (Fm - Fo) / Fm

Functional absorption cross sections ( PSII ) were calculated by fitting the fluorescence transients to a theoretical function describing the relationship between fluorescence and photosynthesis [22].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate functional absorption cross section (σPSII) by fitting fluorescence transients
inputs: Theoretical function relating fluorescence and photosynthesis
